content.content:
1	//
2	//  VCardTransfer.swift
3	//  ContactManager
4	//
5	//  A `Transferable` wrapping a single contact's vCard payload + suggested
6	//  filename. Dragging a row hands one of these to Finder, which receives
7	//  a `.vcf` file named after the contact.
8	//
9	
10	import Foundation
11	import SwiftUI
12	import UniformTypeIdentifiers
13	
14	struct VCardTransfer: Transferable {
15	    /// File-system-safe stem, without the `.vcf` extension. Computed by
16	    /// `suggestedFilename(for:)` so the sanitization is testable.
17	    let suggestedName: String
18	    /// The vCard 3.0 text the file will contain.
19	    let text: String
20	
21	    static var transferRepresentation: some TransferRepresentation {
22	        FileRepresentation(exportedContentType: .vCard) { transfer in
23	            // Write to a per-drag tempdir so multiple drags don't collide on
24	            // a shared filename. Finder copies the file out by the time the
25	            // OS clears the temp dir at next launch.
26	            let directory = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
27	                .appending(path: "vcard-drag-\(UUID().uuidString)")
28	            try FileManager.default.createDirectory(
29	                at: directory, withIntermediateDirectories: true
30	            )
31	            let url = directory.appending(path: "\(transfer.suggestedName).vcf")
32	            try Data(transfer.text.utf8).write(to: url)
33	            return SentTransferredFile(url, allowAccessingOriginalFile: false)
34	        }
35	    }
36	
37	    /// Pure helper: produces a filename stem ("Ada Lovelace") from the
38	    /// contact's `fullName`, falling back to "Contact" for an unnamed one
39	    /// and stripping characters that are illegal on macOS file systems.
40	    static func suggestedFilename(for displayName: String) -> String {
41	        let trimmed = displayName.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
42	        let base = trimmed.isEmpty ? "Contact" : trimmed
43	        // Forward slash and colon are the only path-illegal characters on
44	        // HFS+/APFS; replace them rather than dropping them so two distinct
45	        // names don't collapse into one.
46	        return base
47	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "/", with: "-")
48	            .replacingOccurrences(of: ":", with: "-")
49	    }
50	}
51

## Summary

- [x] No new tests: focus-on-appear is view behavior with no extractable logic
- [ ] Manual: press ⌘N and confirm the cursor lands in First Name ready to type; select an existing contact and confirm focus isn't grabbed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.content:
ID 3368473141 — ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView.swift:97
`onNameFieldFocused()` is invoked immediately after setting `nameFieldFocused = true`. If SwiftUI hasn't actually applied focus yet (timing/layout), the parent flag will be cleared and the view won't retry, leaving the cursor unfocused. Clear the flag only after the field is confirmed focused (e.g., via `onChange` of the `@FocusState`).
---
ID 3368473149 — ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView+Photo.swift:13
These photo action handlers mutate `@State` (`errorMessage`) and call into `ContactStore`/SwiftData. Marking them `@MainActor` makes the UI mutation and model context usage actor-safe regardless of which thread/actor the importer/drop callback happens to run on.
---
ID 3368473153 — ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView+Photo.swift:44
`removePhoto()` updates SwiftData via `store` and mutates `errorMessage`; it should be main-actor isolated like the rest of the UI-facing mutations.
---

content.text:
Three valid comments. My take:
1. **Clear the flag only after focus lands** — right; clearing immediately risks a no-retry miss. I'll clear via `onChange` of the `@FocusState`.
2 & 3. **`@MainActor` on the photo handlers** — they mutate `@State` and use the SwiftData context; explicit main-actor isolation is correct.

Let me apply all three.

gh pr create --title "Drop contacts onto a sidebar group to add them" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
P1 from the audit. Contact rows were draggable (to Finder, as `.vcf`) but the sidebar groups weren't drop targets — dragging a contact onto a group did nothing.

- **`VCardTransfer`** now also carries the contact's encoded `PersistentIdentifier` and exposes it as a plain-text `ProxyRepresentation` alongside the existing `.vcf` `FileRepresentation`. A Finder drag still writes a file; an in-app drop can recover *which* contact moved — no custom UTType needed.
- Each **sidebar group row** gains `.dropDestination(for: String.self)` routing the dropped ids through a new **`ContactStore.addContacts(withEncodedIDs:to:)`**, which resolves them, skips ids that don't match a contact and contacts already in the group, and adds the rest in one undo step (“Add to Group”). Unrelated text drops resolve to nothing and no-op.

Routing through `ContactStore` keeps the membership logic in the data layer and unit-testable (per the repo conventions).

## Test plan
- [x] `make check` — build/lint/format clean; 122 unit tests pass (3 XCUITests flaked locally on app activation only; CI runs swiftformat/swiftlint)
- [x] New `GroupDropTests`: adds dropped contacts; skips existing members and unknown ids (returns the count actually added); empty input is a no-op
- [ ] Manual: drag a contact (or multi-selection) from the list onto a sidebar group and confirm the badge count increases and membership shows in the detail editor

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.content:
ID 3368483359 — ContactManager/Views/SidebarView.swift:58
`.dropDestination` currently returns `true` for any dropped `String`, even when the payload isn’t a contact id (or is empty). This makes unrelated text drops appear “accepted” despite doing nothing, and it diverges from the list’s `.vcf` drop handler which returns `false` for irrelevant drops. Filter to valid encoded `PersistentIdentifier`s and return `false` when none are present before calling `addContacts`.
---
ID 3368483364 — ContactManager/Models/ContactStore.swift:123
`addContacts(withEncodedIDs:to:)` treats arbitrary strings (including empty string) as candidate ids and will fetch *all* contacts even when nothing can possibly resolve (e.g. an unrelated text drop). Since this method is the data-path for drag/drop, it should first validate/normalize the encoded ids (using `PersistentIdentifier.decode(stored:)`) and early-return when none decode; this avoids unnecessary fetches and removes the `?? ""` equality edge case.
---

content.subject: Fix birthday timezone bug
content.description: vCard --MM-DD (no-year) dropped silently; .autoupdatingCurrent causes day shifts across timezones. Fix parsing + use a stable calendar. VCard.swift + ContactsBridge.swift.

content.subject: Size-limit photo read before downscale
content.description: ContactDetailView.swift:259 uses Data(contentsOf:) loading the full raw file before downscale. Route through ImageProcessing.avatarData safe path so a huge image doesn't allocate its full size.

content.subject: Add ShareLink for contact vCard
content.description: VCardTransfer is already Transferable; add a one-line ShareLink to the detail toolbar to share the contact's vCard.

content.subject: ⌘F focuses the search field
content.description: searchable has no keyboard shortcut/focus binding. Wire ⌘F (and/or @FocusState) to focus search. ContactListView.swift:46.

content.subject: Default keyboard focus on new contact's first field
content.description: ⌘N opens the form but focus isn't on First Name; user must mouse there. Add @FocusState default focus.
content.activeForm: Adding default field focus for new contact

content.subject: Sidebar groups as drop destination
content.description: List rows are draggable but dragging a contact onto a sidebar group does nothing. Add a drop destination on group rows that adds membership via ContactStore.setMembership.
content.activeForm: Making sidebar groups a drop target

content.subject: System Contacts import chunking + progress
content.description: ContactsBridge builds an unbounded array on main in a single mega-undo step. Chunk ~500 per save with progress. ContactsBridge.swift:46-56, ContactStore.swift:209-215.

content.subject: Import progress UI
content.description: All three import paths show nothing; a large vCard freezes the UI silently. Add progress feedback. ContentView+Import.swift:24-155.

content.subject: Print / Export PDF
content.description: macOS users expect File ▸ Print of a card or list. Add Print/Export PDF support.

input.command:
cd /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager
git add ContactManager/Models/ContactStore.swift ContactManager/Views/SidebarView.swift
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Address Copilot review on #48: validate dropped ids, reject junk drops

- SidebarView: the group .dropDestination now filters the dropped strings
  to well-formed encoded ids and returns false when none are valid, so an
  unrelated text drop is no longer "accepted" (matches the list's .vcf
  handler).
- ContactStore.addContacts: validate ids via PersistentIdentifier.decode
  up front and early-return when none are well-formed (skips the fetch);
  drop the `?? ""` edge. Matching stays on the canonical encoded string,
  not PersistentIdentifier equality — a decoded id does not compare equal
  to the live persisted one, so == matching returned nothing (caught by
  GroupDropTests).

122 unit tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -q 2>&1 | tail -1
HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
gh api repos/scottdensmore/contactmanager/pulls/48/comments/3368483359/replies -X POST \
  -f body="Fixed in $HEAD — the drop now filters to strings that decode to a valid \`PersistentIdentifier\` and returns \`false\` when none qualify, so unrelated text drops aren't accepted." --jq '.id' >/dev/null && echo r1
gh api repos/scottdensmore/contactmanager/pulls/48/comments/3368483364/replies -X POST \
  -f body="Done in $HEAD — \`addContacts\` now validates the ids via \`PersistentIdentifier.decode\` and early-returns before fetching when none are well-formed, and the \`?? \"\"\` is gone. One caveat I hit: matching by decoded \`PersistentIdentifier\` equality returned nothing for persisted models (a decoded id doesn't compare \`==\` to the live one — caught by GroupDropTests), so matching stays on the canonical encoded string while decode is used purely to validate/short-circuit." --jq '.id' >/dev/null && echo r2
